# Hyperfluid Provider

Manage Hyperfluid platform resources through the Console external API.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# With hfctl brokering credentials, no provider config is required:
#
#   hfctl auth login --service-account ./service_account.json
#   hfctl tf auth        # writes the active profile's credential for Terraform
#   terraform plan       # provider discovers it automatically
#
provider "hyperfluid" {}

# Or point the provider at a service-account JSON explicitly (also settable via
# the HYPERFLUID_CREDENTIALS environment variable, e.g. in CI):
#
# provider "hyperfluid" {
#   endpoint         = "https://console.example.com" # or HYPERFLUID_ENDPOINT
#   credentials_file = "./service_account.json"      # or HYPERFLUID_CREDENTIALS
#   # organization_id is read from the credentials file unless overridden here.
# }
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `credentials_file` (String) Path to the service-account JSON (the same file hfctl consumes). When unset, falls back to the `HYPERFLUID_CREDENTIALS` environment variable, then to the file `hfctl tf auth` writes at `<user-config-dir>/hyperfluid/terraform/credentials.json` (so `hfctl auth login` + `hfctl tf auth` is all that's needed). The secret is never written to state.
- `endpoint` (String) Console API base URL. Falls back to `HYPERFLUID_ENDPOINT`, then `console_url` in the credentials file.
- `organization_id` (String) Organization ID. Falls back to `HYPERFLUID_ORGANIZATION_ID`, then the credentials file.
